qa_pairs.question: What is the recommendation for recompiling code after migrating to the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Even though all files, including binaries, were copied over, codes that did not use the CVMFS software stack or that used absolute paths will have to be recompiled. It is highly recommended to recompile all code, even if it previously used the CVMFS stack on Niagara.

qa_pairs.question: What action is required for virtual Python environments after migrating to Trillium, and what should users do if they previously used Anaconda?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must recreate their virtual Python environments because of the user directory change, as existing environments will not work. If users had Anaconda virtual environments, they should switch to using standard virtual environments instead, as there is no Anaconda module available on Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find support and documentation for the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can refer to the Trillium Quickstart guide, contact support via email at trillium@tech.alliancecan.ca, or take the self-guided 'Intro to SciNet and Trillium' course available at education.scinet.utoronto.ca.
